Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I think there may be a problem here.  This change depends on the existence of include/linux/version.h.  Not sure that exists in all RedHat derived distros. In particular I don't see it in fc18.

Patch Set 1:

So far we also use <linux/version.h> file for RedHat detection:

# check is redhat/suse kernels
AC_MSG_CHECKING([that RedHat kernel])
LB_LINUX_TRY_COMPILE([
                #include <linux/version.h>
        ],[
                #ifndef RHEL_RELEASE_CODE
                #error "not redhat kernel"
                #endif
        ],[
                RHEL_KERNEL="yes"
                AC_MSG_RESULT([yes])
        ],[
                AC_MSG_RESULT([no])
])

Fedora Core it's not exactly RedHat distributive. All checks will fail for it. I didn't see any RHEL kernels with new v3.8 kernel source structure. We will required to change the main check first because RHEL_RELEASE_CODE looked only if RHEL_KERNEL="yes".

Patch Set 1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Your argument persuades me.  I agree we will need more revisions than just this one for 3.8 kernel support.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

If nothing else, the comment of this commit needs to be improved.

What is it about using the kernel version that is incorrect?  How is the RedHat "release version", which seems like a rather obscure bit of information, better than the kernel version?

It may very well be better, but I can't tell why it is without some kind of explanation.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

parents.subject: LU-3460 quota: don't schedule adjust when qsd stopped

commit.subject: LU-3173 build: Incorrect detection of ldiskfs series to use
commit.message:
LU-3173 build: Incorrect detection of ldiskfs series to use

Extract correct upstream kernel release for RHEL kernels and
form it into RHEL_KERNEL_VERSION as
$(VERSION).$(PATCHLEVEL).$(SUBLEVEL)-$(RHEL_RELEASE)
Then use this string for correct ldiskfs series selection.

Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Change-Id: Idda0a4b02a5090b7be6fb81b8f98175559e575aa
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/6056
Tested-by: Hudson
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
